name: Entrevalles - running circular from San Andrés del Rabanedo (PR-LE 82)
name_original: Entrevalles – running circular desde San Andrés del Rabanedo (PR-LE 82)
region_intro: Jogging around San Andrés Del Rabanedo offers access to local parks and dedicated running routes within the municipality. The region features green spaces like Parque Antonio García Valladares, known for its lagoon and shaded areas, providing a pleasant environment for running. Many routes in the area, including those near "Tierras de León," often feature paved surfaces and varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. The blend of urban and natural environments provides diverse options for outdoor exercise. Best jogging routes around San Andrés Del Rabanedo The most popular jogging route is Entrevalles - running circular from San Andrés del Rabanedo (PR-LE 82), a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route is mostly on paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Pathway in León loop from Pinilla, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This route features gentle elevation changes through the local landscape. Local runners also love the Pathway in León loop from El Soto, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through urban green spaces, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Jogging around San Andrés Del Rabanedo is defined by local parks, green spaces, and accessible natural areas, with many routes featuring paved surfaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to more challenging. The routes in San Andrés Del Rabanedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore San Andrés Del Rabanedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in San Andrés Del Rabanedo?
A: There are over 40 dedicated running routes around San Andrés Del Rabanedo, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these trails more than 470 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in San Andrés Del Rabanedo?
A: Many running routes in San Andrés Del Rabanedo feature mostly paved surfaces, especially those within the municipality and local parks like Parque Antonio García Valladares. However, the broader 'Tierras de León' area offers varied terrain, including some with gentle elevation changes, suitable for different running experiences.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
A: Yes, San Andrés Del Rabanedo offers several easy running options. Out of the 44 routes available, 3 are specifically categorized as easy, providing a gentle introduction to jogging in the area. The remaining routes are mostly moderate, with a few more challenging options.

Q: Are there any circular running paths in San Andrés Del Rabanedo?
A: Absolutely. A popular circular running route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052778140/entrevalles-running-circular-from-san-andres-del-rabanedo-pr-le-82" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrevalles - running circular from San Andrés del Rabanedo (PR-LE 82)&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 8.5 km trail is mostly on paved surfaces and takes approximately 56 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some longer running trails in the San Andrés Del Rabanedo area?
A: For those looking for longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pathway in León loop from Pinilla&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.4 km path with gentle elevation changes. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pathway in León loop from El Soto&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10.8 km through urban green spaces.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in San Andrés Del Rabanedo?
A: Within San Andrés Del Rabanedo, you can enjoy the natural space of &lt;strong&gt;Parque Antonio García Valladares&lt;/strong&gt;, also known as Parque de los Patos, which features a lagoon and shaded areas. For those venturing slightly further, the wider León province boasts spectacular landscapes like the mountainous Babia region, the striking reservoir of Riaño, and the impressive stalactites of Cuevas de Valporquero.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the running routes?
A: While jogging in the area, you're close to several significant attractions in León. These include the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5658871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman City Walls of León&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4590991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3952383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcelo Square&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5177760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Santo Domingo Fountain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in San Andrés Del Rabanedo?
A: The running routes in San Andrés Del Rabanedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. Runners often praise the blend of urban and natural environments, the accessibility of local parks, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic views?
A: Many routes, particularly those that venture into the 'Tierras de León' area, offer pleasant scenic views of the local landscape. While specific viewpoints are not highlighted for every route, the natural beauty of the region, including green spaces and varied terrain, provides an enjoyable backdrop for your run.

Q: Is there a good route for a moderate run?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Monolito a los Caidos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.7 km trail with an elevation gain of about 109 meters, offering a good workout. It's a popular choice for local runners seeking a balanced challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in San Andrés Del Rabanedo?
A: San Andrés Del Rabanedo offers a welcoming environment for outdoor exercise year-round. The local parks and green spaces provide shaded areas, which can be particularly pleasant during warmer months. For longer or more exposed routes, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, ideal for comfortable running.
tips: The Calle de las Suertes Greenway, located in San Andrés del Rabanedo, León, is a perfect route for enjoying outdoor activities such as walking, running, or cycling. This greenway follows the Carbosillo Canal, offering a peaceful, natural setting.
